title: Specklinia resupinata
taxonomy: Specklinia resupinata is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Specklinia, which is a part of the subtribe Pleurothallidinae. This subtribe is further categorized under the tribe Epidendroideae, which is one of the largest and most diverse groups of orchids. Specklinia resupinata is a terrestrial orchid, found in tropical regions. The exact classification of Specklinia resupinata reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the orchid family.

intro: Specklinia resupinata, also known as the Resupinate Specklinia, is a species of orchid native to Central and South America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Specklinia resupinata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing.
